differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis secondary to remote abdominal trauma
Portal hypertensive mesenteric vasculopathy (abdominal angina of portal hypertension)
next_steps: Measure serum and ascitic fluid albumin-to-globulin ratio
Perform diagnostic paracentesis to measure SAAG and protein
Order specialized serology for Schistosoma (S. mansoni)
Transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunt (TIPS) evaluation or surgical portal decompression consultation
Consider mesenteric angiography to evaluate for chronic mesenteric ischemia secondary to venous outflow restriction
Liver biopsy (preferably transjugular approach given portal hypertension and coagulopathy)

differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis with portal hypertensive mesenteric vasculopathy
next_steps: CT angiography (CTA) of the mesenteric arteries to rule out chronic mesenteric ischemia
Transjugular liver biopsy for histological evaluation of fibrosis type and architecture
HFE gene mutation testing to investigate elevated transferrin saturation
Stool examination for ova and parasites and Schistosoma serology
Consultation with vascular surgery for evaluation of mesenteric venous/arterial flow and potential decompression
Consultation with hepatology for management of portal hypertension and evaluation for liver transplantation
